title: Arrhenius Equation Explorer
creator: Dan McCreary
subject: AP Chemistry
description: Interactive Arrhenius visualizer showing k vs. temperature and ln k vs. 1/T plots that respond to activation energy, pre-exponential factor, and temperature range sliders.

learningObjectives: Predict how changing Ea and A alters temperature sensitivity of the rate constant.
Interpret the slope of an Arrhenius plot as −Ea/R and connect it to kinetic data.

keyQuestions: Why does a higher Ea increase the slope magnitude on an Arrhenius plot?
How does the frequency factor shift k without changing slope?
commonMisconceptions: Only Ea affects temperature dependence; A has no role.
assessmentOpportunities: Students screenshot both plots and annotate slope and k changes.
Learners compare their curve to the reference curve and explain differences.
